title_semver_calc_visual_semant.en: Semver Calc — Visual Semantic Versioning Calculator

metadesc_interactive_playground_fo.en: Interactive playground for learning Service Worker caching strategies with live code examples and visual diagrams.

subtitle_interactive_guide_to_cach.en: Interactive guide to caching strategies, lifecycle, and update patterns

subtitle_check_the_cache_first_if.en: Check the cache first. If a cached response exists, serve it immediately. Only go to the network if the resource isn't in the cache. This is ideal for assets that rarely change — fonts, images, CSS/JS with versioned URLs.

subtitle_always_try_the_network_fi.en: Always try the network first. If the network responds, cache the result and serve it. If the network fails (offline, timeout), fall back to the cached version. Best for content that needs to be up-to-date — API responses, news feeds, user profiles.

subtitle_serve_the_cached_version.en: Serve the cached version immediately (fast!), but simultaneously fetch the latest version from the network and update the cache in the background. The user sees stale content now but gets fresh content on the next visit. Great for content that updates but isn't time-critical.

subtitle_only_serve_from_the_cache.en: Only serve from the cache. Never go to the network for this request. The resources must have been pre-cached during the install phase. This is the simplest strategy and guarantees offline availability for known assets.

subtitle_always_go_to_the_network.en: Always go to the network. Never use cache. This is equivalent to having no Service Worker for this request, but you can still add logging, analytics, or request modification. Use for requests that must always be live — login, checkout, real-time data.

subtitle_a_service_worker_goes_thr.en: A Service Worker goes through a specific lifecycle: registration → installation → waiting → activation. Understanding this lifecycle is crucial for handling updates correctly and avoiding unexpected behavior.

metadesc_interactive_shadow_dom_vi.en: Interactive Shadow DOM visualizer. Explore open vs closed shadow roots, style encapsulation, slot distribution, ::slotted(), ::part(), adoptedStyleSheets. 5+ live presets.

metadesc_convert_text_to_url_frien.en: Convert text to URL-friendly slugs. Remove accents, special characters, and replace spaces with hyphens.

metadesc_interactive_playground_fo.en: Interactive playground for the Streams API - ReadableStream, WritableStream, TransformStream, backpressure, teeing, and fetch pipelines.

subtitle_create_readable_streams_f.en: Create readable streams from custom sources and consume them chunk by chunk.

subtitle_pull_based_data_is_enqueu.en: Pull-based: data is enqueued on demand when the consumer requests it.

subtitle_writablestream_receives_d.en: WritableStream receives data chunks and processes them through a sink.

subtitle_transformstream_sits_betw.en: TransformStream sits between readable and writable, transforming data as it flows through.

subtitle_backpressure_occurs_when.en: Backpressure occurs when the consumer is slower than the producer. The stream's internal queue fills up, signaling the producer to slow down.

subtitle_tee_splits_a_readablestre.en: tee() splits a ReadableStream into two independent branches. Both receive all chunks. The original stream becomes locked.

subtitle_implement_custom_underlyi.en: Implement custom underlying sources and sinks with full lifecycle control.

subtitle_sink_that_batches_incomin.en: Sink that batches incoming chunks and flushes when batch is full or stream closes.

subtitle_process_fetch_responses_a.en: Process fetch responses as streams for progressive loading and real-time processing.

subtitle_since_we_can_t_fetch_arbi.en: Since we can't fetch arbitrary URLs from a playground, this simulates a streamed response using a ReadableStream.

subtitle_byob_bring_your_own_buffe.en: BYOB (Bring Your Own Buffer) readers allow zero-copy reads into pre-allocated buffers. This is optimal for binary data processing.
